allOf.$comment: interlocutor nodes (source-thinkers) sit outside the domain taxonomy; concept nodes still require a domain.

kind.description: node class; treated as concept when omitted. interlocutors are the thinkers Spengler builds on or breaks with.

targets.description: unit#pN anchors where the term is defined/used.

secondary.description: scholarly-secondary source ids grounding the definition.

items.$comment: Edge-type semantics. STRUCTURAL (no scholarly judgment): instance-of=concrete species of a kind; prime-symbol-of=the Ursymbol of a Culture/soul; phase-of=a stage in the target's life-cycle; derives-from=generated out of a prior ground in Spengler's own system; presupposes=cannot be constituted without the target; exemplified-by=the target is a worked example. INTERPRETIVE (need >=1 citation): contrasts-with=a deliberate Spenglerian opposition; symptom-of=a diagnostic sign the target condition is read off; analogous-to=the signature morphological analogy/contemporaneity (Caesar~Napoleon); narrows=Spengler keeps a borrowed concept's form but deflates its content (e.g. amor fati). INTERLOCUTOR-ONLY (target must be kind:interlocutor): coined-from=term named after/drawn from the thinker; borrows-from=concept taken over; breaks-with=position Spengler rejects/transvalues. related-to=generic, retained only where a sharper type would over-claim. contested-by=reserved.

secondary.description: scholarly-secondary ids; required on evaluative edges (contrasts-with, symptom-of, analogous-to, narrows, breaks-with).
